description.s: Hobson's module system caches evaluated code items for performance. Cache invalidation is event-driven — when an item is updated, its cache entry is deleted so the next
Resolve the name to an item ID (via a name cache)
Check if a cached module exists for that ID
If cache hit → return cached module immediately (fast path: Map lookup)
If cache miss → evaluate the code, cache the result
Cache invalidation happens separately via the event system:
require where you use, not at render time
This is cheap — if the library hasn't changed, you get the cached module. If it has changed, you get fresh code.
Libraries that cache state at module scope break on hot-reload.
When a code item is modified:
Don't cache API at module level. Use
For event listeners, use window-level references for cleanup:
The module system also maintains a name → ID cache for fast
by name. This cache is cleared when an
event fires for a code item, ensuring name changes take effect.

If the same module is required simultaneously (e.g., two views both require the same library during parallel rendering), only one evaluation occurs. A loading promise is stored and shared across concurrent callers.
